response.justification: Bogdan is a strong candidate with 13+ years of professional full-stack experience, a clear match on the primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS, Docker, Kubernetes), and directly relevant AI agent and MCP tooling work in his current role at re:cinq. His track record spans high-throughput microservices, product engineering, and open-source platform development, and he demonstrates solid engineering discipline through TDD advocacy and conference talks. The main gaps are Nest.js (not mentioned anywhere despite being a primary framework), Vitest/Jest not explicitly named (though TDD is practiced), and Kotlin being absent with Java only appearing in older embedded/robotics context — these are learnable but worth probing in interview.
strengths: Current role explicitly includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development, directly matching the AI agent development requirement
Broad cloud and infrastructure experience: AWS CDK at Optilyz, Kubernetes deployment for GISCollective, Docker across multiple roles — covers the containerization and cloud platform requirements well
Consistent TypeScript/Node.js depth across multiple senior roles (re:cinq, Optilyz, RIDE Capital) with high-scale production exposure (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
concerns: Nest.js is entirely absent from the resume despite being listed as the primary backend framework — unclear whether this is a genuine gap or just not mentioned
Kotlin is not mentioned at all, and Java experience is dated and hardware/robotics-focused rather than modern JVM backend services
Testing framework specifics (Jest, Vitest, Playwright) are never named explicitly; TDD practice is referenced but no evidence of the specific tooling the team uses
